item.name: Get a bearer access token via Oauth authentication

exec: // Get the Javascript RSA Library Code from the environment variable
throw new Error("'jslib-jsrsaassign' collection variable not set");
// Get the Client ID (corresponding to the "Key" within the App "IRAT Team Test App" record in Apigee)
throw new Error("'CLIENT_ID' not found in environment variable");
// Sign the JWT using the above jsrsaassign js library
const signedJwt =  KJUR.jws.JWS.sign("RS512", header, payload, pm.environment.get('RSA_PRIVATE_KEY'));
// Set the Client Assertion environment variable to be sent as a parameter to the API call
// Get the access token from the response
// Store this in the environment collection

request.description:
## Client Certificate Authentication

### Setup

Onboarding your application with the API managment portal will allocate you:

- a client_id
- optionally an api key
    

During onboarding you will :

- generate an asyncronous encryption key, 1024 bit size (eg HS512 algorithm)
- keep the private portion very secure
- share the public portion with the API management portal
    

### Creating an access token request

During authentication you will generate a token request with a payload that contains:

- iss: your clientId
- sub: your clientId
- aud: this authentication url
- jti: a random integer
- nbf: current epoch timestamp
- exp: current epoch timestamp plus 5 minutes
    

And a request header that contains:

- alg: the algorithm used for encryption, eg RS512
- typ: always JWT
    

You will encrypt (sign) the request using the private portion of your encryption key and add the sgined result to the request body.

### Sending an access token request

you will send the request body as xform-urlencoded content type:

- grant_type: always clienit_credentials
- client_assertion_type: always :ietf:params:oauth:client-assertion-type:jwt-bearer
- client_assertion: your signed result
    

If your request is successful a json response will contain:

- access_token: the access token you'll need to call the protected resource
    

### Calling the API with the access token

you can call the API by adding the header:

- Authorization: Bearer (your access token)
    

### Getting started

This collection has a demonstration using pre-request and Test to illustrate generating the acccess request and allocating the bearer token for use in the API calls. This is only to demonstrate the encryption and access request generation and allocation of a result to be used in API calls. You'll still need to provide the public portion to the API management portal to actiully get authenticated.

response.name: token denied: invalid client id or client secret
token success
access denied: no access to resource
access: IP Denied Access

request.description:
## When things go wrong

If your result contains an operationOutcome then the request has at least partially failed. The API will respond in one of two ways when things arent quite right.

### 400 status code with a FHIR operationOutcome

Your parameters are malformed in some way or fall outside of an allowed range. The operationOutcome should explain how to correct your request and try again.

### 200 status code with a FHIR bundle containing an operationOutcome

Your parameters are understood but a process behind the API returned an unsatisfactory result. The operationOutcome will explain what couldnt be returned, why is happened and when possible, things you might be able to do to get what you need. The error may be transient so you can retry in its current shape. You can detect transient problems by the opperationOutcome's issue code.

request.description:
for all sucessful read requests the API returns FHIR lists within a FHIR bundle searchset response. You have some filtering options and you can also ask the API to return FHIR resources that are directly related to the list.

> all parameter names are case sensitive 
  

# Things you should know

### Date ordering is always newest first

Lists (not their contents) are always ordered newest first by list date. We present the most recent activity on record first.

### Consolidation

Although the first list represents the most recent activity on record, the data is not consolidated. Evaluating using the information in all lists will provide a greater degree of context and history than the first list alone.

### Safety

We strongly advise the use of the subject value in the _include parameter. This will return a patient resource containing demographic and identifier information describing the patient. This enables cross matching with patient data you have locally. You should use this information to avoid risk and alert your users where there is significant drift between your data and the information provided to you via this API. While a match between NHS number, surname and date of birth is often sufficient each situation is different. We encourage you to apply suitable safety checks in your implementation.

## Required parameters

All queries must include the following items.

### code(s)

You must provide a snomed code for medications & medical devices. You can omit the system portion of the code if you want to.

You have the option of also providing a snomed code for Allergies and adverse reaction with the medications code. To use ths combination you must also be using the optional source parameter to target the patient's GP record. Be advised that the allergy data may not be high quality and may be marked as degraded. This may affect how a clinical decision support tool may interpret the data.

### subject.identifier(s)

This should be the patient's NHS number in all cases by default. When an NHS number is not known the API will attempt to look this up in the Master Patient Index for you.

### status

This should always be `current` when provided and will automatically default to this if omitted.

## Optional parameters

### date(s)

yyyy-MM-dd formatted dates will filter for lists created on that date. The format also allows an le or ge prefix - ie less than or equal to, greater than or equal to. Adding a combination of these also allows you to filter by date ranges. We limit the range to up to two years previous and we dont allow future dates.

### source(s)

Sources typically describe the person or organization that the list was sourced from. Typically used to filter for lists sourced from the Patient's GP Practice. Note that this is not the same as an appointment or encounter filter. The parameter name can be adjusted to target various sources by their properties- normally identifiers. See the 'from gp with all details' example.

### include(s)

You can ask the API for more detail. Use the include parameter to return other resources referenced by the lists References are limited to subject or patient (these two are interchangable terms but patient is more specific), source, items and encounter. You can also use a wildcard to return all available referenced resources.
